sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
The lap starts with one of the most unique corner sequences in Formula 1. Turn 1 is a very long, tightening right-hander that becomes Turn 2 and Turn 3 as it tightens further into a hairpin. Approach flat out and begin braking gently around the 100m mark, gradually increasing brake pressure as the corner tightens. Use a wider entry line, stay in 5th or 4th gear, and trail brake deep while progressively downshifting to 2nd gear by the time you reach the tightest point at the apex.

Be smooth with steering and patient on the throttle, the car will want to understeer straight on as grip fades. Exit to the left to immediately line up for Turn 4, a left-hand kink thats taken in 3rd gear with just a lift or short brake tap. Get back on the throttle early and ride the exit curb slightly.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
Flat or nearly flat, depending on the grip level. Stick to the racing line and hug the inside curb. Maintain momentum — minimal steering input is crucial at this stage of the lap.

Brake hard and late, down to 2nd gear, for this 90-degree left-hander. A late apex allows for earlier throttle. Be careful not to run too wide, as exit traction is tricky and theres limited runoff.

This is a medium-speed rhythm section. Turn in smoothly to Turn 7, a long left-hander in 5th gear, then immediately switch direction to Turn 8, a tighter right, with a downshift to 4th gear. Avoid hitting the inside curbs too aggressively, as they can unsettle the car and chuck you out wide or spin you around.

Brake into Turn 9 and downshift to 3rd, using trail braking to help rotate. Immediately flick into Turn 10 — another short right, requiring throttle modulation to avoid wheelspin. Keep things tight, but prioritise lining up for the acceleration zone. Exit clean and wide.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
Turn 11 begins a long, sweeping right-hander that slowly increases in radius into Turn 12. Start wide, gradually feeding in the throttle while staying in 4th or 5th gear. The corner opens into Turn 13, a fast right-hand kink leading onto the massive DRS straight.

Open the steering gradually and go full throttle early. The exit is slightly banked, so let the car run wide to maximise speed. This section is all about the exit of Turn 13. Sacrifice a tiny bit of mid-corner speed for a clean, early throttle punch to get max benefit for the long straight and the subsequent DRS zone.

At the end of the straight, brake very hard at around the 100m board, all the way down to 2nd gear. This is the tightest corner on the circuit — use the inside kerb slightly, and be patient with throttle application on exit. Easy to lock fronts here, so adjust brake bias forward if necessary.

A short run to the final corner follows the hairpin. Take the corner in 3rd gear. Brake gently while turning in, and aim for a clean exit to maximise your run to the line. Avoid using too much kerb, as it can ruin your exit and leave you vulnerable on the run across the line and into the long turn one.
